text.title: OpenAPI Examples and FastAPI Metadata
text.summary: OpenAPI examples field usage for named request variants, FastAPI app metadata setup, and multi-tool documentation strategy.

section.title: OpenAPI Named Examples

rule.statement: Use the examples field (not example) on requestBody content to provide multiple named variants. Each variant should cover a distinct use case, edge case, or role-based input.
rule.rationale: Named examples appear as selectable presets in Swagger UI, letting developers explore the API's range without reading prose. Multiple examples expose edge cases (e.g. admin vs basic user) that a single example hides.

section.reference: OpenAPI path item with multiple named request/response examples

example.code: paths: /users: post: summary: Create user requestBody: content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/CreateUser' examples: basic: summary: Basic user value: name: "John Doe" email: "john@example.com" with-role: summary: User with admin role value: name: "Admin User" email: "admin@example.com" role: "admin"

antipattern.description: A single example covering only the happy path with no edge cases.
antipattern.reason: Developers copy the example verbatim; untested inputs (special characters, optional fields, boundary values) cause production errors.

section.title: FastAPI Metadata Setup

rule.statement: Configure the FastAPI app with title, description (markdown with auth and rate limit info), version, docs_url, and redoc_url. The description field should contain the authentication header format and rate limit tiers.
rule.rationale: FastAPI generates OpenAPI spec from the app metadata; everything in description appears in Swagger UI and Redoc without any separate documentation maintenance.

section.reference: FastAPI app configuration with full metadata

rule.statement: For interactive try-it-out: use Swagger UI. For clean readable reference docs: use Redoc. For team API design: use Stoplight Elements. Do not maintain separate Markdown docs that duplicate the OpenAPI spec.
rule.rationale: Duplicate documentation drifts. The OpenAPI spec is the single source of truth; rendering tools derive human-readable output from it automatically.

list.item: Swagger UI — interactive, try-it-out, auto-generated from FastAPI
Redoc — clean multi-panel reference, better for reading than testing
Stoplight — visual API design + linting (use for design-first workflow)
Postman — team collaboration, collection sharing, test runner
